corrections.removed: "Compliance / Risk reviews the HireRight background screening report" rewritten to "Compliance / Risk initiates the HireRight background screening report" — document (step 1.3) says Compliance initiates checks, not that they review the report before IT acts.

"AML" removed from Tier 1 module list. Step 3.1 defines Tier 1 as Code of Conduct, InfoSec and Data Privacy only; AML is in the Day-1 assignment list (step 2.5) but not in the Tier 1 completion requirement.
Internal step-ref "ps-23" retained but reworded to "step 3.5" to avoid forward reference to unverified process-step IDs.
AUTOMATED changed to HYBRID. The Workday config is automated but the quarterly access review is manual; HYBRID is the accurate classification.

"detect and remediate any configuration drift" — rewritten to "validates that the configuration has not been circumvented" to stay closer to the evidence column wording without over-specifying an undocumented remediation step.

"HR Operations monitors open probation records in Workday and escalates to the hiring manager and HRBP where no decision has been logged by day 90" — not stated in document. Replaced with the document-supported steps 5.2 and 5.3 only.

"for example a criminal conviction, regulatory bar, PEP/sanctions match, or a discrepancy in employment or qualification history" — specific examples not in document. Removed to leave only what the document states.

"who jointly assess the finding against the organisation's adverse-action policy" and "which may include notifying the candidate, allowing them to respond, and ultimately withdrawing the offer if the finding is disqualifying" — not in document. Replaced with verbatim document text: follow adverse-action procedure.
"or the hiring manager" — document says HR Ops contacts; hiring manager not mentioned in the no-show handling.
"(e.g. portal credentials)" — specific example not in document.

"The shortage is tracked in ServiceNow against the original IT sub-ticket" rewritten to "tracked with IT" (document wording). ServiceNow as the tracking tool is a reasonable inference but the document only says "track shortage with IT". Also removed: "the MDM enrolment is transferred" — not in document.
"originals, within validity dates and from the prescribed list" — not stated in document; inference about I-9 requirements removed.

"to identify acceptable alternative documents" — inference about what "resolve" means, not in document. Also removed: "The original start date is cancelled in Workday; provisioning already initiated is placed on hold" — not in document.
"allowing the hire to proceed under the understanding that signing will be completed before Day 1" and "If a signature is still not received by Day 1, HR Operations escalates" — not in document.
"or cross-border employment arrangement" and "in the hiring country" — elaboration not in document.
"The HRBP coordinates with the organisation's immigration legal advisers to confirm visa status, file sponsor documentation and set a revised target start date" — not in document; inference removed.

"measured via a survey administered by HR Operations at the close of the probation period" — document specifies no 90-day survey vehicle; rewritten to note the document does not describe the measurement mechanism
source changed from 'document' to 'proposed' — the absence of measured actuals is an observation about the document, not a claim the document makes
source changed from 'document' to 'proposed' — document states no significance reasoning; Why it matters block is inference throughout

Claim that HR Ops is 'accountable for controls CTL-NHO-01' removed. CTL-NHO-01 (no system access before background screening clears) is executed by Compliance (screening) and IT (provisioning); the document does not assign accountability for this control to HR Ops. Reference to CTL-NHO-01 in relations.controls also removed.

RACI entries @ps-5:C and @ps-20:C removed. Step 1.5 (equipment list confirmation) assigns ownership to Hiring Manager; the document does not mention HRBP involvement in that step. Step 3.2 (benefits enrolment window) is owned by HR Ops / New Hire; the document does not assign HRBP to that step. Both were drafter inferences not grounded in explicit document text.

Sentence 'The HRBP approves the benefits enrolment window setup (ps-20)' removed — step 3.2 owner is HR Ops / New Hire and the document does not mention HRBP at that step. Sentence 'equipment exceptions (ps-5)' removed — document does not connect HRBP to the equipment list step. Remaining exception coverage (adverse background check, late contract signature) is retained as it is explicitly in §8.

Claim 'Compliance owns controls CTL-NHO-01 and CTL-NHO-03' reworded. CTL-NHO-03 (right-to-work verified on or before Day 1) has step 2.1 owned by HR Ops; the document does not assign CTL-NHO-03 to Compliance. Only CTL-NHO-01 (no access before screening clears) is retained as Compliance's executing control, reworded from 'owns' to 'is the executing party for' since the document does not use ownership language.

Reference @ctrl-3 (CTL-NHO-03) removed from Compliance / Risk relations. CTL-NHO-03 right-to-work verification on Day 1 is performed by HR Ops (step 2.1); document does not assign this control to Compliance.

RACI entry @ps-7:C removed. Step 1.7 (Create employee record in Workday; assign employee ID) owner is HR Ops; the document does not mention Payroll being consulted at this step. The claim was unsupported inference.

Sentence 'Payroll is consulted when HR Ops creates the employee record in Workday at T-5 (ps-7) to ensure the payroll record is correctly seeded' removed entirely. Step 1.7 owner is HR Ops; the document makes no mention of Payroll involvement or of 'seeding' at that step. This was fabricated inference.

Sentence 'The New Hire ... participates in all scheduled check-ins through to the 90-day probation review' removed. Steps 4.2, 5.1 and 5.2 are owned by Hiring Manager (and HRBP for 5.2); the document does not explicitly state the New Hire participates in these check-ins. New Hire's RACI involvement in those steps is a drafter inference, not explicit document text.
[@sys-2, @sys-7] — the document does not state that Workday integrates with Greenhouse or Cornerstone; only that each system is used in the process separately

"archives the closed onboarding ticket at day 95 (step 5.3)" — step 5.3 says HR Ops closes the onboarding ticket and archives evidence, but the onboarding ticket is in ServiceNow (section 5 systems, prerequisites), not Workday
[@sys-1] — the document does not state that Greenhouse integrates with Workday; it lists them as separate systems; the handover is described conceptually, not as a system integration

"passing the confirmed hire record to downstream HR Ops and Workday workflows" — the document says the ATS is used for candidate-to-hire handover and the requisition must be closed in the ATS as a prerequisite, but does not describe an automated record-passing integration to Workday

[@sys-5, @sys-6, @sys-7] — the document does not state that ServiceNow integrates with Okta/AD, Intune/Jamf, or Cornerstone; it only states ServiceNow is used for onboarding tickets and IT/Facilities fulfilment sub-tasks

"employment, criminal and regulatory reference checks" — the document only states HireRight is used for "Background screening"; the specific check types (employment, criminal, regulatory reference) are not listed in the document
[@sys-3, @sys-6] — the document does not state that Okta/AD integrates with ServiceNow or Intune/Jamf
"Intune for Windows devices and Jamf for macOS" — the document lists only "Intune / Jamf | Device enrolment" without specifying which platform covers which OS
[@sys-5] — the document does not state that Intune/Jamf integrates with Okta/AD
[@sys-3] — the document does not state that Cornerstone integrates with ServiceNow; ServiceNow is used for raising L&D sub-tickets, but that is a ticketing action, not a stated system integration
[@sys-1] — the document does not state that DocuSign integrates with Workday
